A whole number is any non-negative integer: 0, 1, 2, 3, …

• 0 – whole number, but not a counting number  
• 2⁄3 – not an integer ⇒ neither counting nor whole  
• 5 – positive integer ⇒ counting number and whole number  
• 8.1 – not an integer ⇒ neither counting nor whole  
• 125 – positive integer ⇒ counting number and whole number

Therefore  
(a) Counting numbers: 5, 125  
(b) Whole numbers: 0, 5, 125
